codeReview.summary: Three pages implemented cleanly: Cannabis Chef (/cannabis-chef), Community (/community), and Testimonials (/testimonials). All three follow established project conventions: server component page files with metadata, feature-specific client/content components, zero border-radius, no box-shadows, Tailwind utility classes, Next.js Image with Unsplash URLs and data-placeholder attribute, proper font classes, and correct design token usage. The Testimonials page correctly uses 'use client' for interactive filtering with useState/useMemo. The split-layout in InfusedDining correctly omits section-padding from max-container per AGENTS.md conventions. Footer links were properly added. New testimonials in constants.ts follow the existing Testimonial interface. Two minor non-blocking observations noted.

issues.description: Uses CSS `direction:rtl` / `direction:ltr` hack to alternate grid column order for initiative items. While this works visually, it's an unconventional approach. A more idiomatic Tailwind pattern would be `lg:order-2` on the image div for odd-indexed items. The `direction` property is designed for text direction (bidi), not layout reordering. It works here because child elements reset to `ltr`, but it could confuse future maintainers and may interact unexpectedly with text-alignment in edge cases.

Filter buttons lack aria-pressed attribute to convey the active/selected state to screen readers. Adding `aria-pressed={activeFilter === cat.key}` would improve accessibility for the toggle filter pattern.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker used a CSS `direction:rtl`/`direction:ltr` hack for alternating grid layouts in the Community page initiatives section. This is a novel pattern not used elsewhere in the codebase. Other alternating layouts in the project don't exist yet as a precedent. If this pattern is undesirable, AGENTS.md could document the preferred approach (e.g., using Tailwind `order-*` utilities for alternating layouts).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/community/CommunityContent.tsx:219 — `lg:[direction:rtl]` applied conditionally on odd-indexed initiative grid items. No other file in the codebase uses this technique.

summary: All three pages (Cannabis Chef, Community, Testimonials) are well-implemented and follow project conventions. Pages use proper Next.js metadata, feature-specific component directories, Tailwind design tokens, zero border-radius, and Next.js Image components. The Testimonials page has working category filtering with correct state management. Footer links were added. TypeScript and lint checks pass. Two non-blocking issues: (1) unconventional CSS direction hack for alternating layouts in Community page, and (2) missing aria-pressed on testimonial filter buttons. Overall quality is good — pass.
